### Post by TasKiNG on 2008-04-11
Hi, Hope you can help as its driving me mad. Have looked at the posts for similar problems but nothing helped.

I use iptables for my firewall and want to host my own website on my server. 
I have installed apache2 and put my site in /var/www 
The website works perfectly from the LAN but is not accessible over the net.
Now if I remove all my iptables rules with sudo iptables -F then it works so it must be iptables.

here is my iptables setup :-

# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.6 on Fri Apr 11 23:47:55 2008
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [142:15693]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [3:362]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [71:6238]
-A POSTROUTING -o eth0 -j MASQUERADE 
COMMIT
# Completed on Fri Apr 11 23:47:55 2008
# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.6 on Fri Apr 11 23:47:55 2008
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [395:45727]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [1247:733039]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [445:84991]
-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,ACK SYN -j DROP 
-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 0 -j DROP 
-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 1 -j DROP 
-A INPUT -p icmp -m icmp --icmp-type 8 -j DROP 
-A INPUT -i eth1 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 6001 -j ACCEPT   
-A OUTPUT -o eth1 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 6001 -j ACCEPT 
-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
# Completed on Fri Apr 11 23:47:55 2008

any help would be appreciated

Thanks

---

### Post by The Cog on 2008-04-12
I think this line is dropping all incoming connection requsts from eth0:
**-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --tcp-flags FIN,SYN,RST,ACK SYN -j DROP **
Also, I think the rules you posted have been edited before posting, which doesn't help.

I suggest you google for good firewall scripts.
This site has some very good info and some sample scripts:
[http://www.linuxhomenetworking.com/wiki/index.php/Quick_HOWTO_:_Ch14_:_Linux_Firewalls_Using_iptables](http://www.linuxhomenetworking.com/wiki/index.php/Quick_HOWTO_:_Ch14_:_Linux_Firewalls_Using_iptables)
